BRET3	-6,1	152	335	44	70	670	Hall and Spakman [2002]	760	120	11	-90	20	101	106	31	3283	14	o	n	E3
BRET2	-7	150	339	41	82	670	Hall and Spakman [2002]	741	75	2	-94	-18	93	68	31	2105	14	o	y	E3
BRET1	-7,3	148	10	37	76	670	Hall and Spakman [2002]	769	65	48	0	-22	83	56	31	1747	14	o	y	E3
aSee the main text for details. 159 oceanic subduction transects are listed. In the column related to upper plate nature (UPN), c means continental, and o means oceanic. In the column related to slab edge, y means yes and n means no. In the column related to upper plate strain (UPS), 0 means neutral, C means compressive, and E means extensive. The scale from active back-arc spreading to active back-arc compression is thus E3-E2-E1-0-C1-C2-C3.
